title: dichanthelium cumbucanum
intro: Dichanthelium cumbucanum, also known as Cumbra's panic grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that is native to the southeastern United States, particularly in Florida. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and ointments, to utilize its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting skin health.
